task_completion.description: Verify that a Table visualization is created using the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Table visualization on the canvas and the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset visible in the data panel or grammar pane.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required columns are displayed in the table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the table headers or the grammar pane (Rows/Values sections) to confirm 'Product Sub Category', 'Profit', and 'Sales' are present.

task_completion.description: Verify that the visualization title is set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the title 'Product Profitability by Sub-Category' displayed above the table visualization or in the visualization properties panel.
Look at the table data to see if negative Profit values are colored Red, OR look for a conditional formatting rules dialog showing '< 0' mapped to Red.

task_completion.description: Verify conditional formatting for Profit >= 0 and < 5000 (Yellow/Amber).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the table data to see if Profit values between 0 and 4999 are colored Yellow/Amber, OR look for a conditional formatting rules dialog showing this range mapped to Yellow/Amber.
Look at the table data to see if Profit values of 5000 or greater are colored Green, OR look for a conditional formatting rules dialog showing '>= 5000' mapped to Green.

task_completion.description: Verify that the workbook is saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Supply_Chain_Profit_Analysis' in the top application title bar, the workbook tab, or a completed 'Save As' dialog.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface, not terminal commands or scripts modifying underlying files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is using the actual Oracle Analytics Desktop application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should match Oracle Analytics Desktop, not a generic spreadsheet tool like Excel, a text editor, or a web search.

integrity.description: Verify the table data is genuinely populated from the dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The table should contain multiple rows of realistic data corresponding to 'Sample Order Lines', rather than just a few manually typed rows.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected filename: 'Supply_Chain_Profit_Analysis.dva' (or 'Supply_Chain_Profit_Analysis' in the UI). Expected visualization title: 'Product Profitability by Sub-Category'. Required columns: 'Product Sub Category', 'Profit', and 'Sales'. Conditional formatting rules for 'Profit': values < 0 must be Red, values >= 0 and < 5000 must be Yellow/Amber, and values >= 5000 must be Green.
